content: If we map the linguistic definition of reflexion as both 'reflection of the external world' and 'internal self-critique' onto LLM iterative self-refinement, we get a novel two-stage self-improvement framework: first, the LLM uses reflexion to ground its initial output in the unstated constraints of the user’s actual context, then it uses Self-Refine to polish the output based on that aligned context. This framework addresses a common flaw of existing Self-Refine pipelines that refine misaligned outputs rather than refining toward what the user actually needs.
